subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: In carrying out work under reimbursable agreements with any State, local, or tribal government, the Secretary, without regard to any provision of law or a regulation—

paragraph.content: may record obligations against accounts receivable from those governments; and
shall credit amounts received from those governments to the appropriate account.

subsection.heading: When Amounts Shall Be Credited.—
subsection.content: Amounts shall be credited within 90 days of the date of the original request by the Service for payment.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;
html:td.html:p: Pub. L. 108–7, div. F, title I (proviso in last undesignated par. under heading “administrative provisions” under heading “National Park Service”), Feb. 20, 2003, 117 Stat. 227.

note.p: The words “Heretofore and hereafter” and “section 1341 of title 31 or” are omitted as unnecessary
